Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.
Explore State Parks
Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission
Sign Up For Our Newsletter
Follow Us on Social Media

Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.

DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania

Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.
Explore State Parks
Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission
Sign Up For Our Newsletter

Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.

DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.
Explore State Parks
Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission

Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.
Explore State Parks
Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission
Sign Up For Our Newsletter
Follow Us on Social Media

Grants
Geology
Volunteer
Featured Activities
Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors

Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.

Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.

Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.

Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.

DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.
Explore State Parks
Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission
Sign Up For Our Newsletter

Featured Activities
Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.
Explore State Parks
Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission
Sign Up For Our Newsletter
Follow Us on Social Media

Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.

DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.

The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.

Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources

Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.

Snowmobiling

News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.
Explore State Parks
Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission
Sign Up For Our Newsletter
Follow Us on Social Media

Grants

Featured Activities
Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.
Explore State Parks
Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission
Sign Up For Our Newsletter
Follow Us on Social Media

Featured Activities
Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

grants.title: DCNR Research Database
grants.combined_text:
Search - DCNR Research Database
DCNR Research Database
Research Application
Help
Welcome to the DCNR Research Database! You can search for research papers in mulitple ways:
1
Map: Use the drawing tools on the map to specify a point, line, or polygon. Additionally, you can choose to select geographic regions such as counties.
            The search will return all research that occured within the chosen location(s).
2
Search Bar: Enter search terms into the search bar to return results based on what you have entered. Include any search terms that are applicable to your desired results.
3
Map & Search Bar: Use the map in conjunction with the search bar for searches that return results matching your search terms within the selected location(s).
Map Search
Geographic Options:
Select
Counties
State Forests
State Forest Districts
State Gamelands
State Parks
State Park Regions
Important Bird Areas
Important Mammal Areas
HUC8 Watersheds
Search
Searching . . .
© 2026 - PA Department of Conservation and Natural Resources

Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.
Explore State Parks
Explore State Forests
Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission

Find More Things to Do In State Parks and Forests
Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.
Snowmobiling
Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.
Snowshoeing
News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.

DCNR conserves and sustains Pennsylvania's natural resources for present and future generations' use and enjoyment.

Overview
As Pennsylvania’s leader and chief advocate for conservation and outdoor recreation, the Department of Conservation and Natural Resources (DCNR) will inspire citizens to:
Value their natural resources
Engage in conservation practices
Experience the outdoors
Learn more about DCNR and its mission

Cross-Country Skiing
Cross-country skiing is a great way to experience the outdoors in winter. Pennsylvania’s state parks and forests offer some of the best places to ski.
Cross-Country Skiing
Snowmobiling
State parks and forests offer thousands of miles and hundreds of acres of designated snowmobile areas for exploration.

Snowshoeing
Snowshoeing has been gaining in popularity in recent years. Snowshoers can travel the many trails of Pennsylvania's beautiful state parks and forests.

News
Find All DCNR News
February 25, 2026
Shapiro Administration Invests More Than $900,000 in 114 Pennsylvania Volunteer Fire Companies to Help Protect Rural Communities from Wildfires
DCNR provides training and equips fire wardens to assist wildland fire protection in communities across the Commonwealth.
February 20, 2026
DCNR Names New Assistant Manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in Southeastern Pennsylvania
Secretary Cindy Adams Dunn announced the appointment of Emma Isaacson as the assistant manager for the Ridley Creek State Park Complex in southeastern Pennsylvania.
February 19, 2026
Pine Creek Rail Trail Selected as Pennsylvania’s 2026 Trail of the Year
The Pine Creek Rail Trail attracts more than 44,000 visitors annually, generating more than $5 million in local economic impact in support of the Commonwealth’s $19 billion outdoor recreation industry.
February 18, 2026
Virtual Meeting Scheduled to Discuss Presque Isle State Park Complex Master Plan
The Department of Conservation and Natural Resources is updating its master plan to guide the future of the complex, which includes Presque Isle State Park, the Tom Ridge Environmental Center (TREC), and Erie Bluffs State Park.
